structured.title: Apostolic Scripture Reading Explained
structured.overview: The discussion explains the apostolic method of interpreting Scripture as Christ-centered, pattern-based, and rooted in the literal meaning of the text. It contrasts typology with allegory, emphasizing that prophecy, shadow, and fulfillment work through real historical patterns authored by God in both Scripture and history. The speaker also references the quadriga approach, listing literal, topological/typological, moral, and eschatological readings, while stressing that interpretation must preserve the original meaning and support the authority of Christ and the New Testament.

apps_results.content:
## Apostolic method
- Christ-centered, pattern-based scripture reading
- Uses types and antitypes
- Predictive prophecy is fulfilled in time

## Not allegory
- Not allegory; keeps literal meaning
- Original text stays the foundation
- Interpretation grows from scripture itself

## Fourfold reading
- Literal: context and culture
- Typological: shadows pointing to Christ
- Moral and eschatological included

## History and scripture
- God authors and shapes both
- Patterns exist in both history and scripture
- Typology is theological, not arbitrary

## Support and examples
- Examples cited: John 3:14-15
- Also 1 Corinthians 10:6
- Plus 1 Peter 3:9, Matthew 21, Colossians 4

## Next steps
- Use this method confidently
- Rejecting it weakens Christ and NT authority
- Continue scanning and refining progress
